title: 10 best herbal teas for oily face
intro_desc: Herbal teas for oily face are infusions made from plants and herbs that are beneficial for reducing excess oil and balancing skin. They contain various compounds such as tannins, antioxidants, and anti-inflammatory agents that help regulate sebum production, soothe irritation, and promote clearer skin. Examples of herbal teas that help with oily face include chamomile, green tea, and spearmint tea. Chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and irritation, while green tea is rich in antioxidants that help regulate sebum production and protect the skin from damage. Spearmint tea, on the other hand, has been shown to reduce androgen levels, which can help reduce excess oil production. By incorporating these herbal teas into their skincare routine, individuals with oily face can experience improved skin texture, reduced shine, and a more balanced complexion, leading to increased confidence and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with oily face because of its natural astringent and antiseptic properties, which help to reduce the production of sebum and calm acne-prone skin. The tea's gentle, soothing effects also help to balance the skin's pH levels, reducing inflammation and tightening pores, thus controlling oiliness and leaving the skin looking smoother and more even-toned.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm tea's high antioxidant content helps reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to clogged pores and acne, leading to a balanced and healthier-looking complexion.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon balm tea can help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne and other skin irritations, while also soothing and calming the skin.
Antibacterial properties: Lemon balm's antibacterial properties can help combat acne-causing bacteria, reducing the formation of blackheads and whiteheads, and promoting a clearer complexion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Lemon balm leaves help balance oil production and reduce inflammation in the skin, making them an excellent addition to teas for acne-prone skin.
Flowers: The delicate flowers of lemon balm contain compounds that have antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can help combat acne-causing bacteria and reduce excess oil.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried melissa officinalis leaves at peak potency and quality.
Measure two teaspoons of dried lemon balm leaves or one tablespoon of fresh leaves per eight ounce cup.
Combine the measured lemon balm with boiling water in a teapot or infuser.
Steep for seven to ten minutes allowing the flavors and oils to meld together harmoniously.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or fine-mesh sieve and serve immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with oily face because it contains menthol, a natural astringent that can help to tighten pores and reduce sebum production. The antibacterial properties of peppermint also help to control acne-causing bacteria, leaving the skin looking clearer and more balanced. Additionally, the calming effects of peppermint tea can soothe irritated skin and reduce inflammation, making it an excellent natural remedy for oily skin prone to breakouts.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Peppermint tea's antioxidants help reduce inflammation and free radical damage, which can contribute to oil production and acne on the skin.
Astringent properties: The astringent compounds in peppermint tea help shrink pores and dry out excess oil, reducing the appearance of pores and controlling sebum production.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint tea's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce redness and swelling associated with acne and other inflammatory skin conditions, helping to soothe and calm the skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Peppermint roots are rich in menthol, which helps to reduce inflammation and calm the skin, making them effective for treating acne-prone and oily faces.
Bark: The bark of the peppermint plant contains flavonoids that help to regulate oil production in the skin, reducing excessive sebum and alleviating clogged pores.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried peppermint leaves for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Combine the measured peppermint leaves with boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot or infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for five to seven minutes or according to package instructions.
Strain the tea into a cup using a spoon or infuser and discard the solids.
Add honey or sugar to taste if desired and enjoy the soothing, caffeine-free herbal tea.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme teas helps with oily face because it has natural astringent and antiseptic properties that help to balance the skin's natural pH levels, reducing excess oil production. The antioxidants present in thyme tea also help to combat free radicals that can cause inflammation and irritation, leading to a clearer and more even-toned complexion. Additionally, thyme tea's antibacterial properties help to combat acne-causing bacteria, resulting in a healthier-looking face with reduced shine.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Thyme tea's antibacterial compounds help to combat acne-causing bacteria on the skin, reducing inflammation and preventing new breakouts.
Antioxidant properties: Thyme's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and contribute to oil production, leading to a more balanced skin tone.

Astringent properties: Thyme tea's astringent compounds help to constrict pores, reducing their size and minimizing the appearance of blackheads and whiteheads, making it an effective remedy for oily skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing redness and inflammation associated with oily skin.
Bark: The bark of thyme contains compounds that help to balance the skin's natural pH levels, which can become disrupted due to excess oil production.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried or fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers for the desired dosage of 1-2 teaspoons.
Boil one quart of filtered water in a teapot or saucepan for a steeping time of 10-15 minutes.
Add the thyme mixture to the boiling water and let it infuse for an additional 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a teacup or thermos.
Serve hot or cool, sweeten with honey if desired, and enjoy the calming properties of thyme.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage teas helps with oily face because of its natural astringent and antiseptic properties. When consumed, the antioxidants and essential oils in sage tea help to balance the skin's natural pH levels, reducing excess oil production and tightening pores. Additionally, sage's anti-inflammatory compounds can help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation associated with oily skin. This results in a clearer, more balanced complexion that is less prone to acne and other skin issues.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Sage tea's antioxidant compounds help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to skin inflammation and acne, reducing the appearance of oily skin.
Anti-inflammatory Properties: Sage tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with acne and oily skin, promoting a clearer complexion.
Astringent Properties: Sage tea's astringent compounds help tighten pores and reduce excess oil production, leaving the skin feeling cleaner and more balanced.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and irritation associated with oily skin, making them an effective ingredient in medicinal teas.
Bark: The bark of the sage plant contains antioxidants and flavonoids that work to balance oil production and soothe acne-prone skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried salvia officinalis leaves for use in tea preparation.
Measure one teaspoon of dried sage leaves or three to four teaspoons of fresh leaves per cup water.
Heat filtered water to boiling point then reduce heat and simmer for seven to ten minutes.
Strain the steeped liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a separate container discarding solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving with sugar or honey if desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marjoram teas helps with oily face because it has natural astringent and antiseptic properties that help to balance the skin's pH levels, reducing excess oil production. The antioxidants present in marjoram also help to soothe and calm irritated skin, while its antibacterial properties combat acne-causing bacteria. By drinking marjoram tea regularly, individuals can promote a healthy and radiant complexion, with reduced signs of oiliness and improved overall skin tone.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Marjoram tea's antimicrobial compounds help to reduce the growth of bacteria on the skin, which can contribute to clogged pores and acne, thereby reducing oiliness.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Marjoram tea's anti-inflammatory compounds help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation that can be exacerbated by oily skin.

Antioxidant properties: Marjoram tea's antioxidants help to neutralize free radicals that can damage the skin and contribute to oil production, promoting a healthier balance of skin oils and reducing acne-causing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of marjoram have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness and inflammation associated with oily skin.
Flowers: Marjoram flowers contain antiseptic compounds that help to control acne-causing bacteria and reduce the appearance of pores on oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried marjoram leaves from the garden or storehouse measuring 2-3 tablespoons for every 8 oz of water.
Rinse the dried marjoram leaves with warm water to remove any impurities and excess moisture.
Combine the marjoram leaves with boiling hot water in a large teapot allowing it to steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth discarding the solids and retaining the flavorful liquid.
Add honey or sugar to taste and enjoy the soothing aroma and calming effects of the marjoram tea within 15-20 minutes preparation time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary teas helps with oily face because of its natural astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's active compounds, such as camphor and borneol, help to tighten pores and reduce excess sebum production, thereby controlling oiliness. Additionally, rosemary's antioxidants and antimicrobial agents work together to combat bacteria that can cause acne, leaving the skin looking clearer and more balanced. By incorporating rosemary teas into your skincare routine, you can say goodbye to greasiness and hello to a healthier, radiant complexion.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial Properties: Rosemary tea's antibacterial properties can help combat acne-causing bacteria on the skin, reducing inflammation and preventing future breakouts.
Anti-Inflammatory Effects: The anti-inflammatory compounds in rosemary tea can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with oily skin conditions like acne and rosacea.

Antioxidant Activity: Rosemary tea's high antioxidant content can help protect the skin from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of oily skin and premature aging.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing acne and inflammation caused by oily skin.
Flowers: Rosemary flowers contain antioxidants and essential oils that help to purify the skin, reduce sebum production, and improve circulation.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried rosemary leaves per 8 ounces of boiling water for a soothing brew.
Add the measured rosemary to a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup and pour in the boiling water.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes to release the aromatic oils and flavonoids from the herb.
Strain the tea into a cup using a spoon or by removing the infuser from the liquid.
Enjoy the flavorful tea immediately, as it's best consumed within an hour of brewing for optimal flavor and benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with oily face because of its natural astringent and antiseptic properties. The tea's potent flavonoids and terpenes work to balance the skin's natural pH, reducing excess oil production and inflammation. Additionally, lavender's calming effects help to soothe pores, tightening them and allowing for a more even release of sebum, resulting in a healthier-looking complexion with reduced shine.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lavender tea's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce redness and inflammation caused by acne, making it an effective remedy for controlling oily skin and preventing further breakouts.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties in lavender tea neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress, which contributes to oily skin and premature aging of the skin, promoting a healthier and more balanced complexion.
Astringent: Lavender tea's astringent property helps to dry out excess oil on the skin, reducing shine and controlling sebum production, leaving the skin looking smoother and less oily.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and irritation in acne-prone skin.
Seeds: Lavender seeds contain a compound called camphor, which has antiseptic and antibacterial properties that can help prevent breakouts and soothe acne.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in sufficient quantity for desired tea strength.
Combine one teaspoon of dried lavender flowers or two teaspoons of fresh lavender flowers with boiling water.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes to release the flavorful and aromatic compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any solids.
Serve the tea hot, sweetened with honey if desired, and enjoy the calming effects.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with oily face because of its natural antibacterial and antifungal properties that help to reduce sebum production, purify pores, and prevent breakouts. The tea's flavonoids also have anti-inflammatory effects that soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation caused by excess oil. By drinking pot marigold tea regularly, it can help to regulate oil production, reduce acne, and leave your skin looking clearer and more balanced.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial Properties: Pot marigold tea's antibacterial compounds, such as calyxin, help combat acne-causing bacteria on the skin, reducing inflammation and preventing further breakouts.
Astringent Properties: The astringent properties of pot marigold tea tighten pores and reduce sebum production, leaving the skin looking smoother and more even-toned.
Anti-inflammatory Properties: Pot marigold tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids, soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with oily skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant contain compounds that help to balance oil production in the skin, making it an effective ingredient for treating oily skin.
Flowers: The flowers of the pot marigold plant have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce redness and inflammation associated with oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried calendula flowers from a trusted source or store-bought packaging for optimal freshness.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried calendula flowers per eight ounces of boiling water for standard steeping.
Pour boiling water over the measured calendula flowers in a tea infuser or heat-resistant cup and let it steep for five to seven minutes.
Strain the tea into a separate container using a tea strainer or cheesecloth to remove any loose flower petals.
Serve the hot or cooled calendula tea immediately, allowing the benefits of pot marigold's anti-inflammatory properties to be fully absorbed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion teas helps with oily face because of its unique ability to balance the body's natural detoxification processes. The tea contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that work to reduce sebum production, which in turn helps to control oiliness. Additionally, dandelion's diuretic properties help to flush out toxins and excess fluids from the skin, leaving it looking clearer and more balanced.
remedy_properties: Diuretic property: Dandelion tea helps to reduce oil production by increasing urine output, which reduces excess sebum on the skin and prevents clogged pores.

Anti-inflammatory property: The flavonoids and terpenes present in dandelion tea have anti-inflammatory effects that help soothe and calm acne-prone skin, reducing redness and irritation caused by oily skin.

Antioxidant property: Dandelion tea's high antioxidant content helps protect the skin from environmental stressors and free radicals that can cause oxidative damage, leading to premature aging and increased oil production.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, dandelion roots help to balance skin pH and reduce inflammation associated with oily skin.
Bark: The bark contains triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to inhibit the production of sebum and reduce acne symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried dandelion flowers and leaves from a trusted source.
Rinse the dandelion material with filtered water to remove any debris.
Combine one teaspoon of dried dandelion petals and one teaspoon of dried leaf.
Steep the mixture in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain and sweeten to taste, enjoying immediately or storing in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow teas helps with oily face because of its unique properties that help balance the skin's natural oil production. The tea contains flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that reduce redness and soothe acne-prone skin. Additionally, yarrow's astringent nature helps to tighten pores and regulate sebum production, reducing the appearance of shine and excess oil on the face. As a result, yarrow teas can help to achieve a clearer, more balanced complexion.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Yarrow tea has astringent properties that help to constrict pores, reducing their size and minimizing the appearance of acne and excess oil on the skin.

Antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow tea's antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds can help to combat acne-causing bacteria and reduce redness and swelling associated with oily skin.
Toner-like properties: The tea's toner-like effects help to balance the skin's natural pH, tighten pores, and remove excess dirt and oil from the surface of the skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, yarrow roots help to reduce inflammation and redness associated with oily skin.
Leaves: High in astringent properties, yarrow leaves help to tighten pores and reduce oil production, making them an effective ingredient for acne-prone skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried achillea millefolium leaves stems and flowers.
Rinse the harvested material with cool running water to remove dirt debris.
Chop the cleaned yarrow into small pieces to increase surface area exposure.
Steep the chopped yarrow in boiling water for 5-7 minutes or longer as desired.
Strain and serve the prepared yarrow tea hot or cold depending on personal taste.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat oily face with herbal teas is by using them as toners. Brew a strong tea from herbs known to control oil production, such as green tea, chamomile, or sage. Allow the tea to cool, then apply it to your face with a cotton ball after cleansing. The tea's tannins will help to remove excess oil and tighten pores. For best results, use twice daily, and store the tea in the refrigerator to extend its shelf life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to oily face that are treated with herbal teas include acne and skin inflammation. For instance, chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne. Similarly, spearmint tea can help regulate hormonal imbalances that can lead to oily skin and breakouts. Additionally, green tea, which is rich in antioxidants, can help reduce inflammation and improve skin complexion. It is important to note that while herbal teas can be beneficial, they should not replace medical treatment, especially for severe cases of acne or other skin conditions. As with any treatment, it is always best to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new regimen.
